title: Matthiola parviflora
taxonomy: Matthiola parviflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Matthiola, which comprises about 40 species of annual and perennial plants. The genus Matthiola is part of the Brassicales order. This species is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its small flowers and slender stems. Matthiola parviflora is also known as the 'small-flowered stock' due to its small, delicate flowers.

The plant's leaves and flowers are traditionally used in folk medicine, often in combination with other herbs, to treat various ailments.
